name: Money Management International
description_short: Money Management International is a credit repair provider based in Stafford, Texas. Rated 5.0/5 with 734 Google reviews, reflecting exceptional customer satisfaction.
description_long:
Money Management International (MMI) is one of the largest nonprofit credit counseling agencies in the United States, headquartered in Stafford, Texas. Founded in the 1950s and accredited by the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) and the Council on Accreditation (COA), MMI operates nationally through a combination of in-person offices, phone counseling, and a robust online platform. The Stafford location serves as both a regional office and part of the organization's broader infrastructure supporting clients across all 50 states.

MMI's core offering is its Debt Management Plan (DMP), a structured repayment program in which MMI negotiates reduced interest rates with creditors and consolidates multiple unsecured debt payments into a single monthly payment. Beyond DMPs, the agency provides HUD-approved housing counseling for homeowners facing foreclosure or navigating the homebuying process, as well as bankruptcy counseling certificates required for both pre-filing and pre-discharge under federal law. All counseling sessions begin with a free, confidential financial review — a meaningful differentiator in a space crowded with fee-first operators. Monthly DMP fees are typically low and capped by state regulation, in line with NFCC standards.

Before engaging MMI, consumers should understand a few practical realities. Enrolling in a Debt Management Plan generally requires closing the credit accounts included in the plan, which can temporarily affect your credit score. DMPs typically run three to five years and require consistent on-time monthly payments to maintain negotiated creditor concessions — missing payments can reset those concessions. MMI also cannot restructure secured debt such as mortgages or auto loans through a DMP, so clients with a mix of secured and unsecured debt may need parallel strategies. Counselors will conduct a full budget review, which is thorough but requires you to share detailed financial information upfront.

With a 4.9-star rating across nearly 600 Google reviews, MMI's client satisfaction appears consistently high — a strong signal for a service category where trust is paramount. Its nonprofit status, decades-long operating history, dual accreditation, and free initial consultations place it among the more credible options in the credit counseling space. That said, prospective clients should verify current fee schedules directly with the agency, confirm their specific creditors participate in MMI's DMP program, and understand that outcomes depend heavily on individual circumstances. MMI is a legitimate, well-regarded resource, but it is not a magic solution — sustainable results require long-term commitment from the client.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Nonprofit agency accredited by both NFCC and the Council on Accreditation (COA) — meaningful third-party vetting
Free initial credit counseling session with no obligation to enroll in a paid program
4.9-star rating from nearly 600 Google reviews, indicating strong and consistent client satisfaction
Operates nationwide with phone and online access, not limited to the Stafford, TX physical location
HUD-approved housing counseling available, covering foreclosure prevention and pre-purchase guidance
Provides federally required bankruptcy counseling certificates (pre-filing and pre-discharge)
cons: Debt Management Plans require closing enrolled credit accounts, which can temporarily lower your credit score
DMPs typically run 3–5 years and require uninterrupted monthly payments — missed payments can void negotiated creditor concessions
Cannot restructure secured debt (mortgages, auto loans) — clients with mixed debt profiles may need additional help beyond what MMI offers
Not all creditors participate in DMP programs, so concession rates and eligibility vary by your specific debt mix
Monthly DMP fees, while regulated and typically modest, add to the overall repayment cost
best_for: Consumers in Stafford, Texas looking for credit repair services
People who prefer working with a local credit repair provider
Individuals with negative items on their credit reports
People preparing for major purchases like home or car buying
diagnosis: MMI is best suited for consumers carrying significant unsecured debt — credit cards, medical bills, personal loans — who want a structured, supervised repayment path with professional creditor negotiation and prefer working with a nonprofit over a for-profit debt settlement firm. The main caveat is that a DMP is a multi-year commitment requiring financial discipline, and clients expecting quick credit score recovery or help with secured debts will need to set realistic expectations.
services: Free initial credit and budget counseling session
Debt Management Plan (DMP) with creditor-negotiated interest rate reductions
Unsecured debt consolidation into a single monthly payment
Student loan counseling and repayment guidance
Financial literacy workshops and educational resources
Online counseling and account management portal
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
